## Analysis set and estimand

This report estimates the **randomised offer effect in this model's fitted analysis set**: children retain their assigned arm, but the repository contains 54 of the 57 randomised children and the model additionally requires its outcome, baseline and any named robustness covariates to be observed. It is therefore an available-case **modified-ITT** analysis, not an unqualified full-population ITT. Extending the causal interpretation beyond the observed analytic data assumes that exclusions and missingness are ignorable given the observed pre-treatment information—that is, they are not differentially related by arm to the unobserved potential outcomes. Any extra complete-case restriction is stated in the model overview.

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
_analysis_set = _csv("analysis_set.csv")
print(
    "_The emitted analysis-set audit is shown below._"
    if _analysis_set is not None
    else "_No `analysis_set.csv` was emitted for this fit; do not infer that all 57 randomised children were analysed._"
)
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
_analysis_set
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
_attrition_bounds = _csv("attrition_bounds.csv")
if _attrition_bounds is not None:
    print("**Attrition sensitivity bounds.** These bounds show how conclusions change under the emitted missing-outcome scenarios.")
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
_attrition_bounds
```

## Results

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print(
    f"This section reports the estimated randomised offer effect on {outcome_label} "
    "within the fitted available-case modified-ITT analysis set "
    "and how much of it is large enough to matter in practice (the ROPE summary). Take "
    "the **direction** from `P(intervention helps)`, not from whether an interval clears "
    "zero."
)
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
tau_summary = _csv("tau_summary.csv")
tau_summary
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
from language_reading_predictors.statistical_models.reporting import odds_string as _odds

if tau_summary is not None:
    _t = tau_summary.iloc[0]
    # New artefacts name the headline direction by estimand and scale. Fall back
    # only so previously fitted reports can still render.
    _prob_ame_pos = float(
        _t["prob_ame_pos"]
        if "prob_ame_pos" in tau_summary.columns
        else _t["prob_tau_pos"]
    )
    _items = f" (≈ **{_t.tau_prob_median * n_trials:+.1f} items**)" if n_trials else ""
    _lab = (
        f", *{_t.direction_label} evidence*"
        if "direction_label" in tau_summary.columns
        else ""
    )
    print(
        f"On the probability scale the average marginal randomised offer effect on "
        f"{outcome_label} is **{_t.tau_prob_median:+.3f}** (risk difference){_items} "
        f"(central 50% credible interval {_t.tau_prob_lo50:+.3f} to {_t.tau_prob_hi50:+.3f}; "
        f"89% {_t.tau_prob_lo:+.3f} to {_t.tau_prob_hi:+.3f}). "
        f"P(intervention helps) = {_prob_ame_pos:.3f} ({_odds(_prob_ame_pos)}{_lab})."
    )
    if str(_t.get("favoured_direction", "positive")) == "negative":
        print(
            f"\n\n_The favoured direction is **harm**: evidence the intervention is "
            f"harmful is *{_t.favoured_direction_label}* (P = "
            f"{_t.favoured_direction_prob:.3f}). The label above is for the "
            f"'intervention helps' claim only._"
        )
    if "tau_prob_hpdi_lo" in tau_summary.columns:
        print(
            f"\n\n_Sensitivity — the 89% highest posterior density interval (HPDI) on the "
            f"probability scale is {_t.tau_prob_hpdi_lo:+.3f} to {_t.tau_prob_hpdi_hi:+.3f}. "
            f"The HPDI is not transformation-invariant, so it is a scale-specific check, "
            f"not a replacement for the equal-tailed interval._"
        )
    if not n_trials_confirmed:
        print(
            "\n\n_Items-scale figures for this outcome are approximate — the test "
            "ceiling (`n_trials`) is unconfirmed._"
        )
    # Monte-Carlo precision of the DERIVED probability-scale AME. The convergence
    # gate scans the model parameters; this reports the sampling precision of the
    # post-processed estimand itself (guarded — appears once the fit carries the
    # columns). Bulk-ESS governs the median; Tail-ESS the 89% interval limits.
    if "tau_prob_ess_tail" in tau_summary.columns and np.isfinite(
        _t.get("tau_prob_ess_tail", float("nan"))
    ):
        _mcse_items = (
            f" (≈ {_t.tau_prob_mcse_median * n_trials:.2g} items)" if n_trials else ""
        )
        print(
            f"\n\n_Monte-Carlo precision of this derived average marginal effect: "
            f"Bulk-ESS {_t.tau_prob_ess_bulk:,.0f}, Tail-ESS {_t.tau_prob_ess_tail:,.0f} "
            f"(the 89% equal-tailed limits are governed by Tail-ESS), median MCSE "
            f"{_t.tau_prob_mcse_median:.4f}{_mcse_items} — the sampling precision of the "
            f"post-processed estimand itself, distinct from the parameter-level gate._"
        )
```

- `tau_logit_*`: posterior median and equal-tailed central interval (coverage `ci_prob`) of $\tau$ on the logit scale.
- `tau_prob_*`: average marginal randomised offer effect on the probability scale (risk difference), averaged over the fitted available-case analysis set per posterior draw.
- `tau_*_lo/hi`, `tau_*_lo50/hi50`: the fixed posterior-band convention (#177, revised 2026-07-17) — the equal-tailed **89%** headline interval (`_lo/_hi`) and the **central 50%** interval (`_lo50/_hi50`, the middle half of the posterior mass; a visual aid, not a threshold), reported alongside the full posterior. Direction is read from `prob_ame_pos`, never from whether a band excludes zero.
- `tau_*_hpdi_lo/hi`: 89% highest posterior density interval (HPDI) at the same coverage — a per-scale sensitivity companion to the equal-tailed interval. The HPDI is not transformation-invariant, so it is reported separately on the logit and probability scales, not mapped between them.
- `prob_ame_pos`: posterior probability that the probability-scale average marginal effect is positive, $P(\operatorname{AME} > 0) = P(\text{intervention helps})$; this is the headline direction probability and `direction_label` is its round-odds evidence label.
- `prob_tau_logit_pos`: posterior probability that the conditional logit-scale treatment coefficient is positive. It is secondary and can differ from `prob_ame_pos` when treatment is moderated across children.
- `prob_tau_pos`: backward-compatible alias of `prob_ame_pos`. New code should use `prob_ame_pos`; the report falls back to this alias only for pre-schema artefacts.

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print(_img("tau_forest.png", "Posterior of the treatment effect (forest, reference line at 0)"))
print(
    "\n_How to read the forest plot:_ the marker is the posterior median and the lines "
    "its credible intervals (thick = inner 50%, thin = 89%). The dashed line at 0 is "
    "'no effect' — posterior mass to its right favours benefit, to its left harm; a "
    "wider line means more uncertainty."
)
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
from language_reading_predictors.statistical_models.reporting import rope_markdown

_rope = _csv("rope_summary.csv")
if _rope is not None:
    print(rope_markdown(_rope, outcome_label))
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
from language_reading_predictors.statistical_models.reporting import (
    rope_sensitivity_markdown,
)

_sens = _csv("rope_sensitivity.csv")
if _sens is not None:
    print(rope_sensitivity_markdown(_sens))
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
_rope.T if _rope is not None else None
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print(_img("rope_summary.png", "ROPE-anchored posterior and P(benefit ≥ δ)"))
print(
    "\n_How to read the ROPE summary:_ the left panel is the posterior effect on the "
    "items scale; the shaded band is the region of practical equivalence (ROPE) — "
    "effects small enough to count as no practical difference. The right panel traces "
    "the probability of a benefit as the 'meaningful' threshold δ rises, so you can see "
    "how likely a *worthwhile* gain is, not just any gain above zero."
)
if _rope is not None:
    # The leading blank line separates the fence from the paragraph printed
    # above; without it Pandoc reads ::: as paragraph text, not a fenced div.
    print(
        '\n::: {.callout-note title="Reading the evidence labels"}\n\n'
        "The round-odds labels (*inconclusive / suggestive / moderate / strong / very "
        "strong*) qualify the **evidence**, never the effect *size*, and are run "
        "separately for the direction and meaningful-benefit claims. At this study's "
        "sample size the point estimate is, on average, magnitude-inflated (a Type-M / "
        "winner's-curse effect), so lead with the interval, not the point.\n\n:::\n"
    )
```

### What the model predicts on the test scale

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
_pred = _csv("predicted_scores.csv")
if _pred is not None:
    _pop = str(_pred.iloc[0].population)
    _status = str(_pred.iloc[0].contrast_status)
    print(_img("predicted_scores.png", "Predicted test scores under each arm, and the items-scale effect with ROPE"))
    print(
        f"\n_How to read it:_ the left panel shows the score the model predicts on {outcome_label} "
        f"(out of {n_trials}) for a **{_pop}** — one distribution per randomised arm, with each "
        "arm's median marked and the items-scale difference annotated. The width of each "
        "distribution is real predictive uncertainty for one child: parameter uncertainty plus "
        "child-to-child spread, so heavily overlapping distributions are expected even when the "
        "average effect is well supported. The right panel is the posterior of the items-scale "
        "average treatment effect with the ROPE band shaded and the three probabilities "
        "(meaningful benefit / negligible / meaningful harm) printed. "
        f"The contrast is a {_status}; the plotted quantities are in `predicted_scores.csv`."
    )
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
_pred if _pred is not None else None
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
if _has("icon_array.png"):
    print(_img("icon_array.png", "Icon array: 100 plausible worlds coloured by the ROPE verdict"))
    print(
        "\n_How to read the icon array:_ each dot is one equally plausible 'world' given the model "
        "and data. Green dots are worlds where the benefit reaches the agreed meaningful "
        "difference δ, red dots are worlds with meaningful harm, and grey dots are worlds where "
        "the effect is smaller than δ in either direction."
    )
```
